summary_one_line: A prefixed attribute whose namespace prefix is NOT the literal 'xmlns' (e.g. 'x', 'xlink'), so the 6-byte ns=='xmlns' gate falls false.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An attribute carrying a non-NULL namespace prefix (ns != NULL) whose first six bytes do NOT spell 'x','m','l','n','s',0 — i.e. a prefixed attribute like 'x:ink' or 'xlink:...' rather than a literal 'xmlns:' namespace declaration. Taking the FALSE side of the ns[0..5]=='xmlns' gate at line 1235-1236 routes execution past line 1303 (ns != NULL search) into the winner-only block.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving cmplog seeds contain prefixed-attribute tokens whose prefix is not 'xmlns': seed f32dee7a138d773b carries 'x:ink' and 'xmlns(xlink' near offset 0x0b-0x1e. At line 1236 the winner takes the FALSE side (W(T/F): T=0 F=6) and then enters the winner-only branch where xmlSearchNs / the undefined-namespace-prefix error path runs (lines 1306-1309 marked [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking naive seeds instead supply a prefix that satisfies the equality, so at line 1236 the loser takes the TRUE side (L(T/F): T=5 F=0) and is consumed entirely inside the 'standard namespace definition' block (lines 1237-1301 marked [L]), never falling through to the winner-only code at line 1303+.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer logged by the byte-wise comparisons against the 'xmlns' literal (ns[0]=='x' ... ns[5]==0) and substitutes the captured operand bytes into the input at the matching offsets, letting cmplog construct an attribute prefix that is byte-distinct from 'xmlns' yet structurally a valid prefixed attribute — solving the multi-byte string roadblock that naive's blind havoc rarely lands on.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the 6-byte equality test ns[0..5]=='x','m','l','n','s',0 inside xmlSAX2AttributeInternal, and the globally blocked side is the false branch.
The blocked side that the winner must reach is the false branch.
At line 1236 the winner takes the false side six times while the loser takes the true side five times.
The winner-only code reached after the false branch is the xmlSearchNs / undefined-namespace-prefix error path at lines 1306-1309.
The loser executes the standard-namespace-definition block (true side, lines 1237-1301) and does not fall through.
A winner-resolving cmplog seed contains a non-'xmlns' prefixed-attribute token 'x:ink'.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one logged CMP operand at offsets where the other operand is found in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; its mutators are havoc + token only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6205 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 51fd9b36079604e3`; the ancestor chain has I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 and depth 4, and the tool reported 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 3, 4.' — the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the 6-byte ns=='xmlns' equality gate where cmplog and naive resolve the false (non-'xmlns' prefix) branch at statistically indistinguishable rates — i.e. naive reaches the prefixed-attribute path as fast as cmplog despite no I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff offsets are scattered/PARTIAL rather than a single concentrated run, so the exact input offset where I2S substitutes the prefix bytes is inferred from the seed token 'x:ink' rather than pinned by a clean per-offset W-vs-L concentration.
